The LEGO Movie 2: The Second Part (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $105,806,508Details
International Box Office $86,500,000
Worldwide Box Office $192,306,508
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,502,183
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $14,958,707
Total $21,460,890
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The heroes of Bricksburg reunite in an adventure to save their beloved city. It's been five years since everything was awesome and the citizens are facing a huge new threat: Invaders from outer space, wrecking everything faster than they can rebuild. The battle to defeat them and restore harmony to the LEGO universe will take Emmet, Lucy, Batman and their friends to faraway, unexplored worlds, including a strange galaxy where everything is a musical. It will test their courage, creativity and Master Building skills, and reveal just how special they really are.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$34,115,335 (32.2% of total gross)
Legs:3.10 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$99,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,303 opening theaters/4,303 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $138,149,111

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 8th, 2019 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: February 6th, 2019 (Wide) (Denmark)
February 6th, 2019 (Wide) (South Korea)
February 7th, 2019 (Wide), released as Лего. Фильм 2 (Russia (CIS))
February 8th, 2019 (Wide), released as LEGO príbeh 2 (Slovakia)
February 8th, 2019 (Wide) (Poland)
Video Release: April 16th, 2019 by Warner Home Video
June 3rd, 2019 by Warner Home Video, released as The Lego Movie 2: The Second Part
MPA Rating: PG for mild action and rude humor. NOTE: EDITED FOR RE-RATE. CONTENT IS DIFFERENT FROM PREVIOUS PG RATED VERSION, BULLETIN NO. 2559 (1/2/19). ONLY THIS EDITED VERSION IS RATED. CHANGE IN RATING DESCRIPTOR ONLY.
(Rating bulletin 2562 (Cert #51974), 1/23/2019)
Running Time: 107 minutes
Franchise: Lego
Cast, crew, or production detail: Gratuitous Cameos
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Alien Abduction, Dinosaurs
Social setting: Alien Invasion, Post Apocalypse, Space Opera
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Target audience: Family Movie
Source:Based on Toy
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Lord Miller, Vertigo Entertainment, Rideback, Warner Bros., Lego Systems A/S
Production Countries: United States, Australia, Denmark, Canada
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

The heroes of Bricksburg reunite in an adventure to save their beloved city. It's been five years since everything was awesome and the citizens are facing a huge new threat: Invaders from outer space, wrecking everything faster than they can rebuild. The battle to defeat them and restore harmony to the LEGO universe will take Emmet, Lucy, Batman and their friends to faraway, unexplored worlds, including a strange galaxy where everything is a musical. It will test their courage, creativity and Master Building skills, and reveal just how special they really are.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$34,115,335 (32.2% of total gross)
Legs:3.10 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$99,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,303 opening theaters/4,303 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $138,149,111
